name: Choco-Cherry Layered Bars
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C). Grease a 13 x 9 inch pan.
2. In a large bowl, beat together the powdered sugar and margarine until light and fluffy. Add the melted chocolate and blend well.
3. Gradually add the flour mixture (lightly spooned into a measuring cup and leveled off) along with baking powder, salt, and milk to the margarine mixture. Blend until combined.
4. Press the dough evenly into the prepared pan.
5. Bake for 15-20 minutes or until set in the center. Allow to cool completely.
6. Chop cherries and drain well, reserving about 2-3 tablespoons of cherry juice.
7. In a large bowl, combine the powdered sugar, margarine, reserved cherry juice, rum extract, and cream cheese. Beat until smooth. Stir in the chopped cherries.
8. Spread the topping evenly over the cooled bars. Drizzle with melted chocolate.
9. Refrigerate for at least 1 hour before cutting into bars. Makes 48 bars.
Note: These bars have a chocolate shortbread-like crust with a creamy cherry filling. They can be baked in advance and frozen to save time.
